rules.explanation_template: This clause imposes a penalty{amount_text} for breach. Section 74 allows only 'reasonable compensation' for actual losses, not punitive penalties. Indian courts routinely reduce excessive penalties. A penalty exceeding 20% of contract value or salary is often considered excessive.
rules.recommendation: Negotiate to cap penalty at actual expected losses (typically 10-15% of contract value). Ensure wording uses 'reasonable compensation' rather than 'penalty'.
rules.legal_reference: Section 74: Party complaining of breach is entitled to receive reasonable compensation not exceeding the amount named, whether or not actual damage is proved.
examples: Employee shall pay 50% of annual salary as penalty for leaving before 2 years.
Contractor liable for liquidated damages of Rs. 10,00,000 for any breach.
Penalty of 3 months' salary for breach of notice period clause.

rules.explanation_template: This clause requires payment{duration_text} in lieu of notice period. While notice period compensation is generally valid, the amount must be reasonable. More than 3 months' salary for notice period breach may be considered excessive under Section 74.
rules.recommendation: Standard practice in India is 1-3 months' notice or pay in lieu. Ensure the amount is proportional to actual impact of departure without notice.
rules.legal_reference: Section 74: Compensation must be reasonable, not punitive. Courts assess whether penalty clause is genuine pre-estimate of loss.
examples: Employee must pay 6 months' salary if failing to serve 3-month notice period.
Payment of 3 months' gross salary in lieu of notice period required.

rules.explanation_template: This training bond requires you to{duration_text} or pay back training costs{amount_text}. While training bonds are legally recognized, they must: (1) involve actual specialized training, (2) have reasonable costs, and (3) have proportional lock-in periods. Courts scrutinize inflated training costs and excessive lock-in periods under Section 74.
rules.recommendation: Verify: (1) Actual training provided matches claimed cost, (2) Bond period is proportional to training duration/cost (typically 1-2x training period), (3) Pro-rated amount if leaving mid-period. Negotiate for documentary proof of training expenses.
rules.legal_reference: Section 74: Training bond penalties must be reasonable estimates of actual training investment, not punitive measures to prevent resignation.
examples: Employee must serve 3 years or reimburse Rs. 5,00,000 training costs.
Training bond of Rs. 10 lakhs for 2-week induction program (likely excessive).
